zh.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles於1539年1月12日出生在英國諾廷加姆爾盧丁頓。她於1556年與Sir Hugh Wardale III在英國林可爾史爾阿福德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1590年1月12日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾阿福德享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾阿福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles于1539年1月12日出生在英国諾廷加姆爾盧丁頓。她于1556年与Sir Hugh Wardale III在英国林可爾史爾阿福德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1590年1月12日在英国英格兰林可爾史爾阿福德享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾阿福德。

nameMeanings.name: Lascelles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one of the large number of places in northern France called Celle, Celles, Lacelle, Leschelle, or Lachelle, all from French celle ‘hermitage’, and at different times occurring both with and without the plural -s.
Variant of   Lacelle   1, a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Laswell
meanings: English: possibly an altered form of   Lascelles  . This surname is also found as   Lacefield  .

variants.name: Lacelle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Andre, Aurele, Benoit, Cecile, Marcel, Marie Paule.
Altered form of French Laselle: habitational name from La Selle, the name of several places in various parts of France. The surname Laselle is very rare in France, found in Marne. Compare Lascelles .

In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Lacelle in Corrèze or numerous minor places called La Celle, all named for a monastic cell or hermitage in their vicinity, from French celle ‘hermitage’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles was born on 12 January 1539, in Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, England. She married Sir Hugh Wardale III in 1556, in Alford, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 12 January 1590, in Alford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51, and was buried in Alford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles est née le 12 janvier 1539, à Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir Hugh Wardale III en 1556, à Alford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 janvier 1590, à Alford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Alford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles wurde am 12. Januar 1539 in Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, England geboren. Sie hat 1556 in Alford, Lincolnshire, England Sir Hugh Wardale III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Januar 1590 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Alford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Alford, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles è nata il il 12 gennaio 1539 nel Ruddington, Nottinghamshire, England. Lei sposato Sir Hugh Wardale III nel 1556, nel Alford, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 gennaio 1590, nel Alford, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Alford, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascellesは1539年1月12日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアルッドイントンで生まれた。1556年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアアルフォードでSir Hugh Wardale IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1590年1月12日にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアアルフォードで51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアアルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles(은)는 1539년 1월 12일 영국 노틴그함실르 루딩턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1556년 영국 링코실르 알퍼드에서 Sir Hugh Wardale III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1590년 1월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 알퍼드에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 알퍼드에 묻혔다.

Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de janeiro de 1590, em Alford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Alford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel Lascelles родилась 12 января 1539 г. в местности руддингтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Hugh Wardale III в 1556 г. в местности алфорд, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 12 января 1590 г. в местности алфорд, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности алфорд, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.
